title: Prove |F_w| is homeomorphic to S^2 (not merely chi = 2)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Gap D.** From the cell structure (5 vertices, 6 edges, 3 squares; $\chi=5-6+3=2$) prove $|F_w|\cong S^2$. **Euler characteristic alone is insufficient** (it cannot by itself exclude non-manifold pinch points, wedges, or non-orientable possibilities with the same $\chi$).

Provide one of:
(i) Show $F_w$ is a closed surface: every 1-cell lies on exactly two 2-cells (no boundary), and the link of every 0-cell is a single circle (manifold point); then show it is connected and orientable, and conclude $S^2$ by the classification of compact surfaces.
(ii) Construct an explicit cellular homeomorphism to a standard CW structure on $S^2$ (e.g. boundary of a triangular bipyramid).
(iii) Compute $H_*(F_w)$ and $\pi_1$: show $\pi_1=1$ (van Kampen) and $H_2=\mathbb{Z}$, so $F_w$ is a simply connected closed surface with $H_2=\mathbb{Z}$, hence $\simeq S^2$.

The genuine content is ruling out non-manifold/wedge points via the link conditions.
